message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature only touches types.py to add normalize_image_bytes helper. Let me know your files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I need to add get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class in types.py. This is a small, additive change (new method + refactor image_to_base64 to use it). If your feature also touches types.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping sections; otherwise this file is mine.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both only need types.py and our changes are small/additive (my get_image_mimetype() method on ImageBlock vs your normalize_image_bytes helper) — true file disjointness isn't possible here. Proposing serialization instead: I'll go first since mine is a single small method addition, implement it, verify, and send DONE. You then pull the updated file and layer your normalize_image_bytes change on top, re-reading the file first so you don't clobber my edit. If you'd rather go first, say so in your reply and I'll wait for your DONE instead.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We both need image_to_base64 in ImageBlock, so full disjoint files isn't possible. Proposal: I'll add a new normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes) helper (static/module-level) that returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing), and change image_to_base64 to: (1) call normalize_image_bytes to get encoded_image/decoded_for_guessing, (2) still call filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) directly (I will NOT introduce a get_image_mimetype method myself), (3) set self.image = encoded_image. This keeps the mimetype-guessing call as a single simple line 'guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)' that you can freely replace with a call to your own get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) method. I'll go first and send DONE with my exact diff so you can layer your get_image_mimetype method + that one-line swap on top without touching my normalize_image_bytes code. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed. You go first with normalize_image_bytes + the one-line filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) call in image_to_base64. Send DONE with your diff and I'll layer get_image_mimetype() + the one-line swap on top without touching your helper.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed: you go first, implement get_image_mimetype(), send DONE. I will wait, then re-read the file fresh and layer normalize_image_bytes on top, keeping your get_image_mimetype call intact inside image_to_base64 (just adjusting it to pass decoded_for_guessing from my helper). Waiting for your DONE now.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I notice our last two messages crossed: I said 'you (agent1) go first', your ACCEPT said 'you (agent2) go first'. To resolve without collision: I will go first now, per your ACCEPT. Implementing get_image_mimetype() on ImageBlock now; I will NOT touch any normalize_image_bytes logic. Please wait and do not edit types.py until you receive my DONE message with the diff.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Done. Added module-level normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) right before class ImageBlock, and refactored image_to_base64 to: encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing); self.image = encoded_image. Tests pass (tests/base/llms/test_types.py, 12 passed). The line 'guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)' is the one you can swap for your get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) call. Re-read the file fresh before editing to avoid clobbering.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) on ImageBlock. It returns cached self.image_mimetype if already set; otherwise guesses via filetype.guess(image_bytes or self.image), caches into self.image_mimetype, and returns it. image_to_base64 now calls self.get_image_mimetype(self.image) instead of the old inline guess block, then does self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image) as before. Diff:
--- a/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
+++ b/llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
@@ -75,14 +75,32 @@ class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
         if not self.image:
             return self
 
-        if not self.image_mimetype:
-            guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
-            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+        self.get_image_mimetype(self.image)
 
         self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)
 
         return self
 
+    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
+        if self.image_mimetype:
+            return self.image_mimetype
+        raw_bytes = image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image
+        if raw_bytes is None:
+            return None
+        guess = filetype.guess(raw_bytes)
+        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
+        return self.image_mimetype
+
     def resolve_image(self, as_base64: bool = False) -> BytesIO:
All 12 tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. You're clear to re-read the file and layer normalize_image_bytes on top now — just pass your decoded_for_guessing bytes into self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) instead of calling filetype.guess directly, and it'll cache correctly.
